items.summary: BOSTON – A Canadian man was sentenced today in federal district court in Boston to embezzling more than $1.4 million from his employer and its clients.

items.title: Theodore Man Sentenced to Prison for Fraud Schemes and Aggravated Identity Theft
items.summary: MOBILE, AL – A Theodore man was sentenced to 32 months in prison for a bank fraud conspiracy, aggravated identity theft, and a wire fraud scheme.

items.title: Wire fraud, extortion indictment highlights dangers of elder fraud
items.summary: A Chinese national has been indicted for his role in a multi-state scheme that swindled more than $1 million by targeting senior citizens.

items.summary: FLORENCE, S.C. —Troy Benjamin Bittner, 55, of Myrtle Beach, was sentenced to more than three years in federal prison after pleading guilty to wire fraud and filing a false tax return.

items.title: Former Assistant Dean Sentenced To 36 Months In Prison For Million-Dollar Embezzlement From Essex County Graduate School
items.summary: NEWARK, N.J. – A former assistant dean of an Essex County graduate school was sentenced yesterday to 36 months in prison for defrauding her former employer of more than $1.3 million.

items.title: Extradited Nigerian National Sentenced to Eight Years in Prison for Business Email Compromise Scheme
items.summary: Okechuckwu Valentine Osuji, 39, a Nigerian national, was sentenced yesterday in New Haven, Connecticut, to eight years in prison for operating a business email compromise scheme out of multiple countries, including the United States.

items.summary: FLORENCE, S.C. — Margaret Ward Heilman, 66, of Darlington County and Katherine Ward Gray, 60, of Florence County, were sentenced to federal prison after pleading guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud.

items.summary: United States Attorney Ronald C. Gathe, Jr. announced that U.S. District Judge John W. deGravelles sentenced Eric Jason Schouest, age 53, of Plaquemine, Louisiana, to 30 months in federal prison following his convictions for embezzlement and bank fraud.

items.summary: FAIRBANKS, Alaska – A federal grand jury in Alaska returned an indictment charging a Fairbanks woman with bank fraud and money laundering related to her alleged theft of funds from a business she formerly worked for as a bookkeeper.

items.title: Nine Members of Multi-State Money Laundering Organization Charged in Wide-Ranging Conspiracy
items.summary: An indictment was unsealed yesterday in Nashville, Tennessee, charging nine members of a multi-state money laundering organization responsible for laundering millions of dollars derived from internet fraud, including business email compromise schemes. The nine defendants were arrested in a takedown coordinated across three jurisdictions.

items.summary: EVAN BOBZIN, 39, of Chester, was sentenced yesterday by U.S. District Judge Kari A. Dooley in Bridgeport to 24 months of imprisonment, followed by three years of supervised release, for theft and tax offenses stemming from a $2 million embezzlement scheme.

items.title: Fresno Man Sentenced for Embezzling Over $1.5 Million from His Employer in Nearly Decade-Long Scheme
items.summary: Gabriel Ruiz De Chavez, 47, of Fresno, was sentenced to two years and nine months in prison for a multiyear embezzlement scheme and ordered to pay over $1.5 million in restitution.

items.title: Arizona Man Charged with Defrauding at Least 54 Victims in Investment Fraud Scheme
items.summary: PHOENIX, Ariz. – Last week, a federal grand jury in Phoenix returned a 19-count indictment against Anthonie Ruinard, Jr., 39, of Chandler, for Wire Fraud and Transactional Money Laundering.

items.title: Warrenton woman sentenced to two years in prison for embezzling over $1M from homeowner associations
items.summary: Rose Kasande Bailey, 60, of Warrenton, was sentenced today to two years in prison for wire fraud involving the pilfering of over a million dollars from homeowner associations (HOAs) she managed.

items.summary: A co-founder of a so-called Christian health care sharing ministry in St. Joseph, Mo., has been sentenced in federal court for his role in an $8 million wire fraud conspiracy that cheated hundreds of members, and for making false statements on a personal tax return.

items.title: Former Arizona Elected Official Pleads Guilty to Embezzlement of More than $38M of County Funds
items.summary: A former Santa Cruz County, Arizona, official pled guilty today to embezzlement, money laundering, and tax evasion for her role in stealing more than $38 million of Santa Cruz County funds between 2014 and 2024 and failing to pay income tax of more than $13 million while she was serving as Santa Cruz County Treasurer.
